title: Local SEO for Lawn Care & Landscaping | Lawn & Land Marketing
metaDescription: Local SEO built for lawn care and landscaping companies. Rank in Google's map pack and organic results so nearby homeowners find and book you first.
schemaDescription: Local search engine optimization built exclusively for lawn care and landscaping companies, covering service-area targeting, dedicated service and city pages, on-page and technical SEO, local citations, Google Business Profile alignment, and rank tracking.
h1: Get Found First When Homeowners Search for a Landscaper Near Them
heroSubhead: When someone searches "landscaper near me" or "lawn care in your town," you want to be the name they see. Local SEO puts your lawn care and landscaping company at the top of Google's map pack and organic results in the areas you actually serve.

conviction.lead: Almost every homeowner reads online reviews before choosing a local business.
conviction.sub: If your lawn care or landscaping company isn't ranking for local searches, homeowners never reach the listing and reviews that would have won the job.

showcaseA.headline: A search presence built around ~the towns you actually serve~
showcaseA.paragraph: We map every service you offer to every city you cover, then build the pages and signals Google needs to rank you. No spray-and-pray keywords, no markets you can't reach. Just dedicated service and city pages, clean on-page optimization, and a technical foundation that makes your landscaping company the obvious local answer.
checklist: Keyword and service-area targeting matched to the work you want more of
Dedicated service pages and city pages for the markets you cover
On-page and technical SEO so Google can crawl, read, and trust your site
showcaseA.imageHint: Screenshot of a Google search results page showing the client's landscaping company ranking in the local map pack plus the #1 organic result for a 'city + landscaper' query

includes.featuredHeadline: Stop being ~invisible~ in the towns where the jobs are
includes.featuredBody: You do great work, but the calls are going to the companies sitting at the top of Google. Ranking for local searches isn't luck or a one-time trick. It's the steady, behind-the-scenes work of pages, listings, and signals all pointing the same direction. We handle that part so you can stay on the truck and out on the job.

cards.title: Keyword Targeting
cards.body: We find the searches homeowners actually use to hire lawn care and landscaping help.

cards.title: Service-Area Pages
cards.body: Dedicated city pages for the markets you cover, so each one can rank on its own.

cards.title: Service Pages
cards.body: A focused page for every service you offer, written to match real search intent.

cards.title: On-Page SEO
cards.body: Titles, headings, and content optimized so Google understands every page clearly.

cards.title: Technical SEO
cards.body: Clean site structure and fast page loads that help search engines crawl and trust your site.

cards.title: Local Citations
cards.body: Consistent name, address, and phone across the directories Google checks.

cards.title: Rank Tracking
cards.body: Ongoing reporting that shows where you rank and how your visibility is trending.

showcaseB.headline: Rankings that turn into ~booked estimates~, not just traffic
showcaseB.paragraph: Showing up is only half of it. We align your pages with your Google Business Profile so the listing homeowners click matches the site they land on, and every page is built to point a visitor toward calling or requesting a quote. The goal isn't vanity rankings. It's a steady stream of local lawn care and landscaping leads that actually fill your schedule.
checklist: Google Business Profile aligned with your site so your listing and pages reinforce each other
Content that answers what local homeowners ask before they hire a landscaper
Pages built to convert searchers into calls and quote requests
showcaseB.imageHint: Screenshot of a rank-tracking dashboard showing keyword positions climbing over time, alongside a Google Business Profile listing with strong reviews

faqQuestions: How long does it take for a landscaping company to start ranking in local search?
What's the difference between local SEO and the Google map pack for lawn care businesses?
Can you help my landscaping company rank in more than one city or town?
Do I need a new website for local SEO, or can you optimize the one I have?
How do you measure whether local SEO is actually bringing in more leads?

cta.headline: Let's get your company ~ranking where the jobs are~
cta.paragraph: Book a free strategy call and we'll look at where your lawn care or landscaping company shows up today, where the gaps are, and what it would take to own the searches in your service area.
cta.note: Free strategy call. No pressure, no obligation.

faqAnswers: It depends on your market: population, competition, keyword difficulty, and the momentum you already have all play a part. SEO is the slowest part of your digital marketing, but over time it becomes the most effective, and the hardest for competitors to take from you. As a general range, expect 6 to 12 months before organic search is making a real, steady impact.

They're related but separate. Local SEO is the broader work of getting your website to rank in the regular (organic) search results for your services and service areas. The map pack is the block of three businesses with the little map that shows near the top of local searches, and it's driven mainly by your Google Business Profile, your reviews, and proximity. You want both: the map pack for fast, high-intent visibility, and organic SEO for the staying power underneath it. We work them together.
Absolutely. We target every area you actually serve, building out your site and local presence so you show up across your whole service footprint, not just the town your office sits in.

For local SEO to really work, it needs the right foundation, which is why we build new. We don't optimize or rebuild existing sites, because too often the problems are buried in the code where they quietly hold your rankings back. Our from-scratch builds are made to rank and to keep adapting as search and AI change, so your SEO stands on solid ground from day one.

We track the top of the funnel with tools like Google Search Console and Google Analytics to watch impressions, clicks, and rankings climb. Then we tie it to real results: form submissions, and phone calls tracked through a dedicated number on your site, so you see actual leads, not just traffic.

faqQuestions: How much should a landscaping company budget for Google Ads each month?
What's the difference between Google Ads and Local Services Ads for lawn care?
How do you keep my ad spend from being wasted on the wrong searches?
How quickly can a new Google Ads campaign start bringing in leads?
Can you target only the specific towns and zip codes my crews actually serve?

cta.headline: Let's find the searches ~worth paying for~
cta.paragraph: Book a free strategy call and we'll look at the high-intent searches in your service area, where your current spend is leaking, and what a Google Ads program built for booked jobs would look like for your company.
cta.note: No pressure, no contract to book the call. Just a straight look at the opportunity.

faqAnswers: It depends on the season and your local competition. You should lean in during your peak months when search volume is highest, and ease off in the slower stretches, usually the holidays toward the end of the year. Google Ads should never be set-it-and-forget-it: the budget is a strategic call based on your goals, market, services, and seasons, and we set it with you.

Regular Google Ads (search ads) put your business at the top of the results when someone searches for your service, and you pay per click. Local Services Ads are the Google Guaranteed listings that sit even higher, show your reviews, and charge per lead instead of per click. LSAs tend to bring high-intent calls, while search ads give you more reach and control. For most landscapers the strongest setup uses both, and we weight them to your market.

A few ways. We build out negative keyword lists so you stop paying for searches that'll never become customers (think jobs, free, or DIY), tighten match types so your ads show for genuine intent, and review the real search terms regularly to cut what isn't converting. We also lock targeting to the areas you serve and track conversions, so your budget keeps flowing to what's booking actual jobs.
Usually within about two weeks. From there it tends to build, as Google learns who your best customers are and we make optimization adjustments to sharpen the campaign.
Yes. Google Ads lets us get far more precise than SEO, targeting specific counties, cities, or even individual zip codes, so your spend stays on the areas your crews can actually reach.

faqQuestions: Do Facebook and Instagram ads actually work for lawn care and landscaping companies?
What's the difference between boosting a post and running a real Meta ads campaign?
How do Meta ads compare to Google Ads for getting landscaping leads?
How do you target only the homeowners in my service area?
What kind of ad creative do you use if I don't have professional photos or video?

cta.headline: Let's get you in front of the ~right homeowners~.
cta.paragraph: Book a free strategy call and we'll walk through your service area, your busy seasons, and what a Facebook and Instagram ad program could look like for your lawn care or landscaping company. No pitch, no pressure, just a clear plan.
cta.note: Free strategy call · No contracts discussed · No-pressure conversation

faqAnswers: Yes. Close rates run lower than Google, because people scrolling social aren't actively searching for you, but Meta makes up for it on volume: we can put your business in front of far more local homeowners. Used well, it's a powerful way to create demand rather than just capture it.

Boosting is the quick promote-this-post button: easy, but with almost no control over who sees it or what it's meant to achieve. A real campaign, built in Meta's Ads Manager, lets us set the objective, target the right local homeowners, test multiple creatives and offers, and optimize for actual leads. Boosting spends money; a real campaign is built to bring back jobs.

Google leads are higher quality, because the person is actively searching for your exact service. Meta is interruption: you're stopping someone mid-scroll, so you have to give them a reason to stop, usually a strong offer. In our experience, qualified Google Ads leads tend to close around 60% to 75%, while Meta is closer to 10% to 20%. Meta still earns its place by reaching people Google never will and keeping you in front of your market.

Meta's AI does a lot of the heavy lifting now: it reads your ad's creative and copy to find the people most likely to respond in the areas you serve. On top of that, we add simple qualifying questions to the lead form, which filter out the folks who aren't a real fit before they ever reach your phone.

No problem. We can create our own branded graphics, and even realistic, on-brand images, through our AI systems, designed to match your company and proven to drive impressions and conversions. You don't need a photographer to run great-looking ads.

faqQuestions: How long does it take to start ranking in the Google Maps map pack for lawn care searches?
What is the difference between Google Business Profile management and local SEO?
Can you manage my Google Business Profile if it is already claimed and verified?
How do you get more Google reviews for a landscaping company without breaking Google's rules?
Will managing my Google Business Profile help me show up in nearby towns I service?

cta.headline: See where you ~rank on Maps~ right now
cta.paragraph: Book a free strategy call and we will look at your Google Business Profile and your current map-pack position across your service area, then show you exactly what is holding your landscaping company back.
cta.note: No pitch deck, no obligation, just a clear look at your local visibility.

faqAnswers: It depends on things like your local competition, the current state of your Google Business Profile, how many reviews you have and how steadily they come in, your proximity to the cities you want to rank in, and the keywords you're targeting. The good news: map-pack rankings can move much faster than website SEO, anywhere from about one month to six.

Local SEO is about ranking your website in Google's regular search results. Google Business Profile management is about your map listing: the profile that feeds the map pack and Google Maps, including your categories, services, photos, reviews, and posts. They reinforce each other, but they're different levers, and a strong local presence needs both. We manage them as one system.
Yes. As long as you're the owner of the profile, you can grant us the access we need to manage it, with no need to start over or re-verify anything.

We set up an automated system that invites your customers to leave a review, ideally right after you or your crew finishes the job, while the experience is fresh. We can also reach past customers through text and email campaigns. It's all permission-based and within Google's guidelines, never bought or faked.

Yes, though proximity is a big factor: the farther a town is from your address, the more competitors sit between you and those customers. We optimize your profile to reach as far as it credibly can. And if you have, or open, a real location in one of those farther markets, a second profile there is the legitimate way to compete for it, and we'll help you set it up the right way.

Which review sites do you manage besides Google for lawn care companies?
Do you respond to reviews for me, or do I have to write the replies?
Can my best reviews be displayed directly on my landscaping website?

cta.headline: Ready to ~out-review the competition~?
cta.paragraph: Book a free strategy call and we'll look at where your reviews stand today, where your competitors are beating you, and exactly how we'd build a steady flow of 5-star reviews for your lawn care or landscaping company.
cta.note: Free strategy call, no pressure, no obligation.

faqAnswers: The best reviews come from asking at the right moment, ideally right after you finish a job and the customer's happy. We make that effortless with automated text and email reminders that go out on your behalf, so the ask is gentle, well-timed, and never naggy.

We alert you right away and ask for the context behind it. First, we try to get the review removed if it breaks Google's policies. If it can't be removed, we craft a professional, measured reply using your side of the story and Google's best practices, so future customers see a business that handles feedback with class.

Google is the gold standard for reviews, so that's where we focus our energy, and where yours is best spent too. It carries the most weight for both your reputation and your local rankings, so we prioritize it above the rest.
We handle it. Every review, positive or negative, gets a timely, on-brand reply from us on your behalf, so you stay engaged with your customers without adding one more thing to your plate.

Yes. Through our software, your top reviews are embedded right on your site, built to be crawled by search engines and read by potential customers, so your reputation goes to work the moment someone lands on the page.

title: CRM & Marketing Automation for Landscapers | Lawn & Land Marketing
metaDescription: CRM and marketing automation for landscapers: capture every lead, answer in seconds, and follow up automatically with text-back, reminders, and review requests.
schemaDescription: CRM and marketing automation for lawn care and landscaping companies: one pipeline with instant lead response, follow-up sequences, missed-call text-back, appointment reminders, review requests, and full contact and pipeline management.
h1: Every lead captured, tracked, and followed up, automatically
heroSubhead: A CRM and automation system for landscapers and lawn care companies that organizes every lead in one place, answers each one in seconds, and keeps following up until they book. No more leads slipping through the cracks while you're out on a job.

conviction.lead: Respond inside 5 minutes or watch the lead go cold.
conviction.sub: The Lead Response Management Study found you're far more likely to qualify a new lead when you reach out within five minutes instead of thirty. Automation makes that the default for every inquiry, day or night.

showcaseA.headline: A CRM that ~never sleeps on a lead~
showcaseA.paragraph: Every form fill, call, and message gets an instant, personal response, then a steady sequence of texts and emails until the homeowner books or opts out. We wire it all into one pipeline so nothing falls through, whether you're on a mower or off the clock.
checklist: Instant text and email reply the moment a lead comes in
Missed-call text-back so no call goes unanswered
Multi-step nurture sequences that follow up for you
showcaseA.imageHint: Screenshot of a CRM pipeline view with automated text/email sequences firing on a new lead card

includes.featuredHeadline: Most of your lost jobs ~never get a second touch~
includes.featuredBody: You're slammed running crews, so leads pile up and follow-up slips. By the time you call back, the homeowner already hired the landscaper who answered first. Automation closes that gap for you, so the slow days stop costing you booked work.

cards.title: Instant Lead Response
cards.body: Every new lead gets an immediate text and email reply.

cards.title: Missed-Call Text-Back
cards.body: Unanswered calls trigger an automatic follow-up text.

cards.title: Follow-Up Sequences
cards.body: Scheduled SMS and email touches that nudge leads to book.

cards.title: Appointment Reminders
cards.body: Automated reminders help cut no-shows on scheduled estimates.

cards.title: Review Requests
cards.body: Happy clients get an automatic prompt to leave a review.

cards.title: Lead Reactivation
cards.body: Dormant and old leads get re-engaged with fresh outreach.

cards.title: Pipeline & Reporting
cards.body: Every lead tracked in one CRM with clear reporting.

showcaseB.headline: Turn the leads you already have into ~more booked estimates~
showcaseB.paragraph: You're already spending money to generate lawn care and landscaping leads. Automation makes each one work harder by responding fast, following up relentlessly, and reviving the old inquiries sitting dead in your inbox. More of your existing demand turns into jobs on the calendar.
checklist: Catch leads after hours, on weekends, and mid-job
Win the homeowner by being the first to respond
Reactivate old leads and fill gaps in your schedule
showcaseB.imageHint: Screenshot of a reporting dashboard showing leads responded to, reminders sent, and reviews requested

faqQuestions: What is marketing automation and how does it work for a landscaping business?
How fast can an automated system respond to new lawn care leads?
Will automated follow-up texts and emails feel personal to homeowners?
Can you connect automation to the CRM or software I already use?
How does missed-call text-back help me book more estimates?

cta.headline: Stop letting good leads ~slip away~
cta.paragraph: Book a free strategy call and we'll map where leads are leaking out of your pipeline and how speed-to-lead automation can turn more of them into booked jobs.
cta.note: Free strategy call. No obligation, no pressure.

faqAnswers: Marketing automation is the system that makes sure no lead slips through the cracks. For your company that means automatic texts, emails, alerts, reminders, and notifications that fire at the right moment, so every homeowner who reaches out gets a fast, consistent response without you having to remember to send it.
Instantly. The moment a lead comes in, the system responds, and that speed is the whole point: the business that replies first usually wins the job.
Yes. Every message is written for your industry and in your company's voice, so it reads like it came from you, not from a generic template.

It depends on what you're running. Our automations are built into the CRM we set up for managing your leads, but we can often adapt the process to work with the CRM and systems you already use. We'll find the right fit on your strategy call.

When you miss a call, the caller instantly gets a friendly text from your business. That keeps them from dialing your competitor next, opens a text conversation while they're still interested, and buys you time to call back without losing the job.
